chief_complaint: A 50-year-old male presented with acute weakness after accidentally ingesting hydrogen peroxide.
history: The patient initially experienced retrosternal burning immediately after intake and developed acute weakness a few hours later. He had no past medical history and was not taking any medications. His social history was unremarkable.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed weakness in the left upper and lower limbs.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: normal. Imaging: multiple cerebral infarcts caused by air emboli on neuroimaging.
correct_diagnosis: Cerebral air embolism due to hydrogen peroxide poisoning
differentials: Cerebral air embolism due to hydrogen peroxide poisoning
correct_ranking: Cerebral air embolism due to hydrogen peroxide poisoning
